reviews.review_title: TO ANYONE WHO WANTS TO LEARN
reviews.review_description:
this game is great and will be greater once the developers smooth everything out but to any new players I urge you to hop off your vr and take 10 minutes to watch a tutorial mainly on stances and technique because the learning curve is just to hard on quest and there's barley any instruction to anyone who wants to learn ima link a yt vid if it'll work/ rumble vr 101 (pose guide)
https://youtu.be/6v3XV8kywXM?si=DKdkYPvkbM-MQJ8C

I love the game and I think it has beautiful potential, but, please, you need to take yourself more seriously and make a few quality of life improvements. Once you have a game thats simi polished it will take over the market. I personally think the name needs to be changed and I think you should make it a little easier for newer players to get. All in all great game I do recommend to any players. And Dev, please you are the backbone of this game, it is up to you to make the game good enough to succeed on mass. Free games are often played by kids so make it easier to understand. Good luck. ~Simple Studios.

For people who think the game is glitchy and never registers your moves, it's because you always have to be very precise with your form.   The placement, distance, and rotation of your hands have to be correct which sounds like it's wayyyy too hard, but it's just muscle memory.

 When you open the game, there's a bunch of icons that show you your moves (eg.  Straight, wall, etc), so just do it for a few minutes, so you don't struggle in actual matches. ALSO, don't click "straight" as the first move to learn or you'll think it's broken. You have to learn how to spawn stuff like a wall or ball, then you use straight ON the stuff you spawned

reviews.review_title: Cannot play the game and do not know how
reviews.review_description: I don’t know how to play and I don’t understand the controls. Please hire me a coach.

developer_response.body:
There's a menu with a list of the poses you can do. Click on a pose you want to learn and align your hands to the correct rotation and position that the pose shows. Some poses apply modifiers, such as straight, which pushes an object forward, while others summon rocks, like cube. 

Once you have learned the moves and you are ready to fight, you can join the queue to fight other players in a round.  If the poses feel too hard, you can activate easy mode. However, even in easy mode it's still gonna take some time to learn all the poses and get the muscle memory. The good thing is that all the poses are pretty much the same as the real Rumble game, which will be coming to Quest soon so your skills will transfer over.

reviews.review_description: Nothing works I can't even move

developer_response.body: Try putting new batteries in both of your controllers. If that doesn't work try uninstalling and reinstalling the game.

reviews.review_description: Can’t even tell what your hands are doing do not get.

A game of this is just 2 people staring at each other for 20 mins failing to cast a thing and it only ends one one quits or kills them self with the 1 wall they managed to spawn. 

The game has a menu of spells to learn and even with your hands in the exact position it shows it fails to register more often than not, when you try to kick/uppercut a wall it barely moves an inch but if your too close it will damage you but not the opponent if by miracle you manage to hit them.

The game could be good but it’s got a long way to go

developer_response.body: gotta make sure your hands are in the correct rotation as well. Try enabling easy mode it might be funner.

This junk azz!!!! 👎

I’m so glad that someone has FINALLY decided to take up the role of hero in our tale and bring us something like Rumble. I mean until now there hasn’t been anything or even any attempts that I have found that were even remotely close to being like Rumble. This game is hard ngl but so is Rumble (I hear) so it is true to the real game in that way. Man, I been looking like every week before this game came out. It’s a good game that you can only get good at by playing it more and more just like the real game. Obviously the graphics aren’t going to be that good but that is not to be expected considering it just came out and it’s still in its early stages. Im expecting this game to eventually look better BUT not to look incredible since it is a fan game. Overall, I can see this game going places and attracting quite a bit of attention. Yo, you, yeah you, dev, listen, parry is a bit overpowered and what I mean by that is most of the time people I’ve fought don’t even mean to parry and they just have their hands close to there face or extended and it counts as a parry and so they then just block all damage I thought I delt. Yeah, so maybe make parry a little more locked behind a bit of skill and that would fix this. Thanks for reading. Dev, I believe in you.
How do you play with friends????

developer_response.body: You can join a private queue by entering a name for the queue into the text box, then hit the join button under the text box. When someone else, i.e. your friend, enters the same name that you put in and hits the join button, they will go into the same private queue as you and the match will start.
